episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 8, 2026
episodes.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 family (Sol, Terra, Luna) re-prices frontier intelligence as it self-cannibalizes on cost; Google scraps and rebuilds Gemini 3.5 Pro for a July 17 launch; Anthropic's 19-day Fable 5 export-control shutdown sets a dual-use regulatory template; and Chinese open weights keep setting the price floor beneath a $725B capex arms race.

episodes.title: Capital Markets Cross-Asset Radar — July 8, 2026
episodes.summary: A hawkish Fed and a fresh Iran oil shock pull the same way: the June FOMC minutes show officials floating rate hikes with PCE revised to 3.6%, the 30-year yield tops 5%, Brent jumps past $78, and equities shrug while Bitcoin and the 60/40 bear the strain.

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 8, 2026
episodes.summary: The real institutionalization story is the digital-dollar stack — regulated stablecoins, $7B in tokenized Treasuries, bank-chartered issuers, and Coinbase's yield-arbitrage — all racing the GENIUS Act's July 18 rule deadline, even as Bitcoin trades like leveraged Nasdaq and catches no haven bid during the Iran strikes.

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 8, 2026
episodes.summary: Energy became the base layer of power in real time this week: US strikes on Iran put the Strait of Hormuz back in play and spike oil, NATO's Ankara summit turns rearmament into $50B+ of procurement, and America's AI buildout is throttled not by chips but by transformers, switchgear, and an interconnect queue that tariffs are taxing.

episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 9, 2026
episodes.summary: Today is the day OpenAI actually opens the door. GPT-5.6 — the Sol, Terra, and Luna family that was announced back on June 26th and has been sitting in a tightly gated preview — expands its rollout today, and with the expansion we finally have the thing that was missing at announcement: numbers.

episodes.title: Capital Markets Cross-Asset Radar — July 9, 2026
episodes.summary: Here's the concrete evidence. The implied probability of a Fed rate hike at the September meeting jumped to roughly 70 percent, up from 58 percent just the prior session. That's a twelve-point move in a day, and it's not about term premium anymore.

episodes.title: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure — July 9, 2026
episodes.summary: Begin with NVIDIA, because it frames everything. Its most recent quarter was 81.6 billion dollars in revenue, with data-center revenue up 92 percent year over year to 75.2 billion. And through June, it began ramping its next-generation Vera Rubin platform into production.

episodes.title: AI Products & Applications — July 9, 2026
episodes.summary: The most telling product development this week isn't a new coding agent — it's a product whose entire job is to manage the coding agents you already have. Journi launched DevOS, a platform to measure, govern, and optimize AI-assisted software development across tools like Claude Code and Cursor.

episodes.title: Enterprise Technology & Software — July 9, 2026
episodes.summary: The defining question in enterprise software right now is brutally simple: does AI eat SaaS, or does it feed it? For the past several months the market has been trading on the fear that autonomous agents make seat-based software obsolete — why pay for a thousand licenses when an agent does the work?

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 9, 2026
episodes.summary: The news is that the SEC is preparing to propose its first major crypto rule as soon as this month — an initiative the agency is calling "Regulation Crypto." The substance is what matters: it would create temporary exemptions from securities registration for developers first issuing crypto investment contracts, allow…

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 9, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the number that should reframe how you think about data-center economics. In PJM — the grid operator covering thirteen states and the largest power market in the country — the capacity auction for the 2026-2027 delivery year cleared at 329.17 dollars per megawatt-day, across all zones, a record.

episodes.title: The Throughline (daily synthesis) — July 9, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the master variable, because it's hiding in three different briefings wearing three different costumes: the Strait of Hormuz.

episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 10, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I spent the whole briefing on GPT-5.6's benchmarks and the pricing divergence between OpenAI and Anthropic. Today I want to move up a layer, because the most important competitive action this week isn't happening at the model or the price point.

episodes.title: Capital Markets Cross-Asset Radar — July 10, 2026
episodes.summary: Here's the evidence that reframes it. The June employment report showed the economy added just 57,000 jobs, against a consensus near 115,000, so barely half.

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 10, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with market structure, because this is the load-bearing question for the whole US digital-asset industry.

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 10, 2026
episodes.summary: Start at Hormuz, because the situation is more precarious than the headline "the President called the deal over" conveys, and the precision matters.

episodes.title: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure — July 10, 2026
episodes.summary: Here are the facts. Micron, Samsung, SK Hynix, and the memory-focused ETFs are all down more than 20 percent from their recent closing highs.

episodes.title: AI Products & Applications — July 10, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I argued the story had moved from "does the agent work" to governance and interoperability, and I built it around MCP going generally available as the standard socket for enterprise data.

episodes.title: Enterprise Technology & Software — July 10, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I framed enterprise software as a re-rating along the pricing model, consumption platforms winning, seat-based incumbents defending, security minting new demand.

episodes.title: The Throughline (daily synthesis) — July 10, 2026
episodes.summary: And today that thread is unusually clear, so let me name it up front: the market has started pricing a ceiling on the AI buildout. For two years the AI story was a one-way capacity race, more compute, more memory, more capex, more power, all of it unquestioned.

episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 11, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I argued the competition had moved off the model layer and onto compute, that the labs had become each other's landlords and the moat was turning into a capital-markets game. That's still the deep structure.

episodes.title: Capital Markets Cross-Asset Radar — July 11, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the oil reversal, because it's the fresh fact.

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 11, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the regulatory calendar, because there are now two distinct clocks running and they're both live this month.

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 11, 2026
episodes.summary: Start at Hormuz, because the reversal is the fresh fact and the skeptic's discipline matters more here than anywhere. The United States and Iran have signed a memorandum of understanding and opened indirect talks in Qatar over restoring the flow of shipping, and traffic through the Strait is recovering.

episodes.title: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure — July 11, 2026
episodes.summary: Here are the results. SK Hynix priced its ADRs at 149 dollars, raised 26.5 billion dollars, and popped 13 percent on debut to close around 168. That listing is the largest ADR offering in market history, bigger than Alibaba's 2014 debut, and the book was oversubscribed more than seven times.

episodes.title: AI Products & Applications — July 11, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I argued the frontier had moved to agents taking actions, and I built it around Claude gaining write access to Microsoft 365, an assistant that could finally send the email and move the meeting.

episodes.title: Enterprise Technology & Software — July 11, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I framed the sector as a two-sided squeeze: application vendors punished for the fear that AI erodes their seats, infrastructure spenders punished for capex they may not be able to finance, a tape where almost nothing was being rewarded. That's still the backdrop.

episodes.title: The Throughline (daily synthesis) — July 11, 2026
episodes.summary: The single biggest cross-domain theme today has a name: proof. Across almost every briefing, the market stopped extending the AI buildout unlimited credit and started demanding evidence of return on the specific dollar.

episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 12, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I told you the competition had relocated onto two new battlegrounds, distribution and the public markets, and that the IPO race would force the whole "who's winning" argument out of the demo and into an audited S-1. That's still the frame.

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 12, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with what Circle actually got, because the details matter more than the headline. The OCC granted final approval for Circle to operate First National Digital Currency Bank, National Association, under the trade name Circle National Trust.

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 12, 2026
episodes.summary: Start at Hormuz, because the reversal there is violent and specific. The June memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran, the piece of paper I warned you was only a piece of paper, has effectively collapsed.

episodes.title: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure — July 12, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the Chinese end, because it's the one with geopolitical weight. Reuters reported on July 7th that DeepSeek is designing its own AI chip, and the crucial detail is the specialization: it's an inference chip, built to serve a trained model to users, not to train one.

episodes.title: AI Products & Applications — July 12, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I built the briefing around ChatGPT Work, the agent you delegate a whole job to, and I said the products that win won't be the ones that can do the most but the ones you can trust to do it unwatched.

episodes.title: Enterprise Technology & Software — July 12, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I framed the sector as a live argument, the first real counter-narrative to "AI kills software" colliding with ChatGPT Work as the first credible outside threat to the incumbents' core seat. That debate is still unresolved, and the late-July earnings calendar will adjudicate it.

episodes.title: The Throughline (daily synthesis) — July 12, 2026
episodes.summary: The single biggest cross-domain theme today has a name, and it isn't capability. It's control. Across almost every briefing, the marginal story stopped being about who is most powerful or most advanced, and became about who holds governing authority, who's in control, and what it costs to seize it.

episodes.title: Capital Markets Cross-Asset Radar — July 12, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the oil reversal, because it inverts last week's story. A week ago I told you crude had rolled over on Gulf de-escalation, and I framed the fading inflation impulse as the fresh fact. That framing is now wrong, and the correction matters.

episodes.title: AI in Biopharma R&D — July 12, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the single most important structural shift of 2026, because it reframes everything else. The industry has stopped buying AI-discovered molecules one at a time and started licensing the models themselves.

episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 13, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I told you the frontier race had become four simultaneous contests, on intelligence, distribution, inference compute, and political capital. Today the story is that a fifth front just opened, and it's the courtroom.

episodes.title: Capital Markets Cross-Asset Radar — July 13, 2026
episodes.summary: Gold fell on a war morning. On a day when a fifth of the world's seaborne oil transit was declared closed by one of the combatants, gold dropped one point four percent to around four thousand sixty-four dollars an ounce, slipping below forty-one hundred.

episodes.summary: The military facts first, because the specifics tell you the escalation logic. The U.S.

episodes.title: AI Products & Applications — July 13, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I gave you the tripod agentic software stands on, cheap enough to run, connected enough to be useful, governed enough to trust.

episodes.title: Enterprise Technology & Software — July 13, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I told you the incumbents had found their defensive high ground in agent governance.

episodes.title: AI in Biopharma R&D — July 13, 2026

episodes.title: The Throughline (daily synthesis) — July 13, 2026
episodes.summary: Today's biggest cross-domain story has a name, and the name is the chokepoint. Every briefing today, without exception, turned on a narrow passage that someone controls and everyone else must pay to cross.

episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 14, 2026

episodes.title: Capital Markets Cross-Asset Radar — July 14, 2026
episodes.summary: Here's what happened. Korea Investment and Securities published a second-quarter profit estimate for SK Hynix roughly eight percent below consensus, citing slower-than-expected HBM4 shipments and the company's heavy reliance on HBM contracts.

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 14, 2026
episodes.summary: Flows first, because there's real news. The U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF complex snapped a ten-day outflow streak with about two hundred twenty-two million dollars of net inflows, the largest daily haul in two months, per Investing News.

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 14, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the tanker attack, because the details carry the escalation logic. The Mombasa and the Al Bahiya were hit in the strait's southern shipping lane, inside Oman's territorial waters, and the dead crewman was Indian, with eight more injured. India summoned Iran's envoy within hours.

episodes.title: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure — July 14, 2026
episodes.summary: The crash first, with the mechanics in order. Korea Investment and Securities published a second-quarter operating-profit estimate for SK Hynix roughly eight percent below consensus, citing slower-than-expected HBM4 shipments and the company's concentrated reliance on HBM contracts.

episodes.title: AI Products & Applications — July 14, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I told you voice had crossed from demo to default and Search had rebuilt itself around answers.

episodes.title: Enterprise Technology & Software — July 14, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I gave you the two-hundred-thirty-four-billion-dollar number, Gartner's estimate of application spend exposed to agentic arbitrage.

episodes.title: AI in Biopharma R&D — July 14, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the FDA's real-time clinical trials program, because the details are further along than most of the industry has registered.

episodes.title: The Throughline (daily synthesis) — July 14, 2026
episodes.summary: The biggest cross-domain story of the day has a name, and the name is rationing. Not scarcity in the abstract, the briefings have been documenting scarcity for months, but the specific moment when scarcity stops being a statistic and becomes someone's decision about who gets what.

episodes.summary: Yesterday I told you the hierarchy at the frontier is set by who can say no to whom on compute.

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 15, 2026
episodes.summary: The OCC granted Circle its national trust charter on July 10th, making USDC the first stablecoin operating under direct federal supervision, per the industry reporting that surfaced this week.

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 15, 2026
episodes.summary: First the war's overnight ledger. CENTCOM ran a seven-hour strike operation into early Wednesday, hitting dozens of military targets along Iran's coastline, the fourth consecutive night, while the reinstated naval blockade holds ships off Iranian ports, per CNBC and NPR. Iran's Revolutionary Guard says it struck U.S.

episodes.title: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure — July 15, 2026
episodes.summary: ASML first, with the numbers in order.

episodes.title: AI Products & Applications — July 15, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I told you the AI coworker had become a three-way product war in a single week, and that the usage data pointed its real market at the back office rather than the IDE.

episodes.title: Enterprise Technology & Software — July 15, 2026
episodes.summary: Yesterday I argued the platform vendors had decided the scarce resource in enterprise AI is implementation, and were buying and building consultancies, Microsoft's Frontier Company, OpenAI's Northslope acquisition, to seize the services layer from the integrators.

episodes.title: AI in Biopharma R&D — July 15, 2026
episodes.summary: Start with the deals, because the pattern is the news, not any single headline number.

episodes.title: The Throughline (daily synthesis) — July 15, 2026
episodes.summary: The single biggest cross-domain story today has a name, and the name is the state. Not the state as legislator, passing laws from a distance, but the state as counterparty, sitting inside the stack, holding equity, curating customer lists, granting charters, permitting load, and reviewing molecules in real time.

episodes.title: Frontier AI Lab Competition — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: One theme organizes almost everything that happened this week: the frontier is repricing. Challengers are attacking from below with aggressive token pricing, while the two incumbent flagships — OpenAI's GPT-5.6 and Google's Gemini 3.5 Pro — are arriving late, gated, or both.

episodes.title: Capital Markets Cross-Asset Radar — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: Markets are caught this week between two forces pulling in opposite directions: the best inflation print in three years, and an oil shock that could unwind it.

episodes.title: Digital Money, Crypto, and Market Structure — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: The story of this week is that the stablecoin business model — earn the float, keep the float — is being repriced in real time, from three directions at once: a consortium that wants to give the yield away, a regulator finalizing the rulebook by Friday, and an incumbent whose distribution partner just became its…

episodes.title: Strategic Power, Energy, and Institutional Change — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: Everything worth discussing today is a variation on one idea: power in both senses of the word.

episodes.title: Semiconductors & Compute Infrastructure — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: This is audit week for the AI supercycle. June handed the chip complex a 1.7-trillion-dollar rout, per Bloomberg's tally, driven by memory oversupply fears and profit-taking — and now the two companies with the deepest visibility into real demand are on the stand. ASML testified yesterday. TSMC takes the stand today.

episodes.title: AI Products & Applications — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: If you want one sentence for the week, it's this: the agent stopped being a demo and became a line item.

episodes.title: Enterprise Technology & Software — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: Enterprise IT budgets are a fixed pie, and this week the market finally got hard evidence of who's losing their slice to AI infrastructure. That evidence arrived as the worst single day in IBM's hundred-plus-year trading history, and it reorganizes how to read every software print between now and September.

episodes.title: AI in Biopharma R&D — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: Today's organizing question is who gets to own the engine — because the last two weeks have redrawn the map of who is building drug-discovery AI, who is renting it, and who is trying to skip the whole partnership dance entirely.

episodes.title: The Throughline (daily synthesis) — July 16, 2026
episodes.summary: The single biggest cross-domain story today is chokepoint economics. Across every briefing in the stack, the price of things is being set less by how much exists and more by who controls the narrow point it has to pass through.
